sql: WITH product_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, name, avg_disbursed, avg_interest, loan_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_disbursed ASC) AS row_num FROM product_loans) SELECT product_id, name, avg_disbursed, avg_interest, loan_count FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1

question: List the borrowers who have taken loans with the highest average EMI amount, along with their credit score and the number of loans?
sql: WITH borrower_loans AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, AVG(l.emi_amount) AS avg_emi, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, full_name, avg_emi, loan_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_emi DESC) AS rank FROM borrower_loans) SELECT rb.borrower_id, rb.full_name, rb.avg_emi, rb.loan_count, b.credit_score FROM ranked_borrowers rb JOIN borrowers b ON rb.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE rb.rank = 1

question: Find the loan products with the highest average requested tenure, along with the number of applications and the average interest rate?
sql: WITH product_tenures AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(la.requested_tenure) AS avg_tenure, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest, COUNT(la.application_id) AS app_count FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, name, avg_tenure, avg_interest, app_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_tenure DESC) AS row_num FROM product_tenures) SELECT product_id, name, avg_tenure, avg_interest, app_count FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1

question: Show me the top 3 cities by the average credit score of borrowers, along with the number of borrowers and the total amount disbursed?
sql: WITH city_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit, COUNT(b.borrower_id) AS borrower_count, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.city), ranked_cities AS (SELECT city, avg_credit, borrower_count, total_disbursed, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_credit DESC) AS rank FROM city_borrowers) SELECT rc.city, rc.avg_credit, rc.borrower_count, rc.total_disbursed FROM ranked_cities rc WHERE rc.rank <= 3

question: What are the loan products with the highest average outstanding amount, along with the number of loans and the average interest rate?
sql: WITH product_outstandings AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.outstanding) AS avg_outstanding, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, name, avg_outstanding, avg_interest, loan_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_outstanding DESC) AS row_num FROM product_outstandings) SELECT product_id, name, avg_outstanding, avg_interest, loan_count FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1

question: List the borrowers who have taken loans with the highest average DPD, along with their credit score and the number of loans?
sql: WITH borrower_loans AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, AVG(l.dpd) AS avg_dpd, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, full_name, avg_dpd, loan_count, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_dpd DESC) AS rank FROM borrower_loans) SELECT rb.borrower_id, rb.full_name, rb.avg_dpd, rb.loan_count, b.credit_score FROM ranked_borrowers rb JOIN borrowers b ON rb.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE rb.rank = 1

question: What is the top 3 loan products with the highest average disbursed amount per borrower city, considering only borrowers with a credit score above 600?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, b.city, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE b.credit_score > 600 GROUP BY lp.product_id, b.city) SELECT lp.name, rp.city, rp.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_products rp JOIN loan_products lp ON rp.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE rp.row_num <= 3 AND lp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM borrowers WHERE credit_score > 600))

question: List all borrowers who have taken at least two loans with different loan products, along with their total disbursed amount and the average interest rate of the loan products they've taken, ranked by the total disbursed amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT lp.product_id) > 1) SELECT rb.borrower_id, rb.full_name, rb.total_disbursed_amount, rb.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_products))

question: Find the loan product with the highest average disbursed amount per borrower, considering only borrowers who have taken at least two loans with the same loan product, and rank the products by the average disbursed amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE la.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications GROUP BY borrower_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT application_id) > 1) GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rp.product_id, rp.name, rp.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM borrowers))

question: What is the total disbursed amount for each borrower city, along with the average credit score of the borrowers in each city, ranked by the total disbursed amount in descending order, considering only borrowers who have taken at least one loan?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT b.city, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS ntile FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT rc.city, rc.total_disbursed_amount, rc.avg_credit_score FROM ranked_cities rc WHERE rc.city IN (SELECT city FROM borrowers WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications))

question: List all loan products with their average disbursed amount per borrower, along with the total number of borrowers who have taken each product, ranked by the average disbursed amount in descending order, considering only borrowers who have taken at least one loan with the product
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rp.product_id, rp.name, rp.avg_disbursed_amount, rp.num_borrowers FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM borrowers))

question: Find the borrower with the highest total disbursed amount, along with their credit score and the average interest rate of the loan products they've taken, ranked by the total disbursed amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score) SELECT rb.borrower_id, rb.full_name, rb.credit_score, rb.total_disbursed_amount, rb.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_products))

question: What is the average credit score of borrowers who have taken at least two loans with different loan products, along with the total number of such borrowers, ranked by the average credit score in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.credit_score, COUNT(DISTINCT la.product_id) AS num_products, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY b.credit_score DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.credit_score HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT la.product_id) > 1) SELECT rb.borrower_id, rb.credit_score, rb.num_products, rb.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_products))

question: List all loan products with their total disbursed amount, along with the average credit score of the borrowers who have taken each product, ranked by the total disbursed amount in descending order, considering only borrowers who have taken at least one loan with the product
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS ntile FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rp.product_id, rp.name, rp.total_disbursed_amount, rp.avg_credit_score FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM borrowers))

question: Find the borrower city with the highest average credit score, along with the total number of borrowers in the city and the average disbursed amount per borrower, ranked by the average credit score in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT rc.city, rc.avg_credit_score, rc.num_borrowers, rc.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_cities rc WHERE rc.city IN (SELECT city FROM borrowers WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications))

question: What is the total disbursed amount for each loan product, along with the average interest rate of the product and the total number of borrowers who have taken the product, ranked by the total disbursed amount in descending order, considering only borrowers who have taken at least one loan with the product
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rp.product_id, rp.name, rp.total_disbursed_amount, rp.avg_interest_rate, rp.num_borrowers FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM borrowers))

question: What are the top 3 cities with the highest average loan amounts, along with the number of borrowers and the average credit score for each city?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT city, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_loan_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(la.requested_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT city, avg_loan_amount, num_borrowers, avg_credit_score FROM ranked_cities WHERE row_num <= 3

question: Find the loan products with the highest acceptance rate, considering only products with more than 100 applications, and rank them by their average requested amount per borrower?
sql: WITH product_acceptance AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, COUNT(CASE WHEN la.status = 'approved' THEN 1 END) AS approved_count, COUNT(la.application_id) AS total_count, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_requested_amount FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name HAVING COUNT(la.application_id) > 100), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, name, approved_count, total_count, avg_requested_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY approved_count / total_count DESC) AS row_num FROM product_acceptance) SELECT name, approved_count, total_count, avg_requested_amount FROM ranked_products ORDER BY row_num

question: Which borrowers have the highest total repayment amount, and what is their average credit score and employment type?
sql: WITH borrower_repayments AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type, SUM(r.paid_amount) AS total_repayment FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_repayment, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_repayment DESC) AS row_num FROM borrower_repayments) SELECT full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_repayment FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num <= 10

question: Find the top 5 loan products by average disbursed amount, and calculate the percentage of loans that are non-performing assets (NPA) for each product?
sql: WITH product_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN l.npa_flag = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS npa_count, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS total_loans FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, name, avg_disbursed_amount, npa_count, total_loans, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_disbursed_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM product_loans) SELECT name, avg_disbursed_amount, npa_count, total_loans, ROUND(npa_count::numeric / total_loans, 2) AS npa_percentage FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 5

question: What are the cities with the lowest average credit score, and what is the average loan amount and number of borrowers for each city?
sql: WITH city_credit AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_loan_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) ASC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT city, avg_credit_score, avg_loan_amount, num_borrowers FROM city_credit WHERE row_num <= 5

question: Find the borrowers who have applied for loans with the highest total requested amount, and calculate their average credit score and employment type?
sql: WITH borrower_requests AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type, SUM(la.requested_amount) AS total_requested FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_requested, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_requested DESC) AS row_num FROM borrower_requests) SELECT full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_requested FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num <= 10

question: Which loan products have the highest average requested tenure, and what is the average disbursed amount and number of loans for each product?
sql: WITH product_tenure AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(la.requested_tenure) AS avg_tenure, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, COUNT(l.loan_id) AS num_loans, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(la.requested_tenure) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT name, avg_tenure, avg_disbursed, num_loans FROM product_tenure WHERE row_num <= 5

question: Find the top 3 employment types by average loan amount, and calculate the percentage of borrowers with a credit score above 700 for each employment type?
sql: WITH employment_loans AS (SELECT b.employment_type, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_loan_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN b.credit_score > 700 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS good_credit_count, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS total_borrowers, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(la.requested_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.employment_type), ranked_employment AS (SELECT employment_type, avg_loan_amount, good_credit_count, total_borrowers, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY avg_loan_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM employment_loans) SELECT employment_type, avg_loan_amount, good_credit_count, total_borrowers, ROUND(good_credit_count::numeric / total_borrowers, 2) AS good_credit_percentage FROM ranked_employment WHERE row_num <= 3

question: What are the cities with the highest average loan amount, and what is the average credit score and number of borrowers for each city?
sql: WITH city_loans AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_loan_amount, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(la.requested_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT city, avg_loan_amount, avg_credit_score, num_borrowers FROM city_loans WHERE row_num <= 5

question: Find the borrowers who have the highest total repayment amount, and calculate their average credit score and employment type, considering only borrowers with more than 2 loans?
sql: WITH borrower_repayments AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type, SUM(r.paid_amount) AS total_repayment, COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_id) AS num_loans FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.employment_type HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_id) > 2), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_repayment, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_repayment DESC) AS row_num FROM borrower_repayments) SELECT full_name, credit_score, employment_type, total_repayment FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num <= 10

question: Which borrowers have the highest credit score among those who have applied for a loan with an amount greater than the average requested amount, and what is their average annual income
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, b.annual_income, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY b.credit_score DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id WHERE la.requested_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications)) SELECT rb.full_name, rb.credit_score, AVG(rb.annual_income) AS avg_annual_income FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.rank = 1 GROUP BY rb.full_name, rb.credit_score

question: Show me the loan products with the highest average repayment amount, considering only loans with a repayment status of 'paid' and a payment mode of 'online', and rank them by their average interest rate
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(r.paid_amount) AS avg_repayment_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_id ORDER BY AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE r.status = 'paid' AND r.payment_mode = 'online' GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rl.name, rl.avg_repayment_amount, rl.row_num FROM ranked_loans rl WHERE rl.row_num = 1

question: Find the borrowers who have applied for a loan with a requested amount greater than the average requested amount of all loans, and what is their average credit score, considering only borrowers with a credit score greater than the average credit score of all borrowers
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_requested_amount, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id ORDER BY b.credit_score DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id WHERE la.requested_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications) AND b.credit_score > (SELECT AVG(credit_score) FROM borrowers) GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.credit_score) SELECT rb.full_name, rb.credit_score, rb.avg_requested_amount FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.rank = 1

question: Which loan products have the highest average disbursed amount, considering only loans with a disbursed amount greater than the average disbursed amount of all loans, and what is their average interest rate
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS ntile FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans) GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rl.name, rl.avg_disbursed_amount, rl.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_loans rl WHERE rl.ntile = 1

question: Find the top 5 borrowers with the highest total repayment amount, considering only loans with a repayment status of 'paid', and what is their average credit score
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, SUM(r.paid_amount) AS total_repayment_amount, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(r.paid_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE r.status = 'paid' GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name) SELECT rb.full_name, rb.total_repayment_amount, rb.avg_credit_score FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.row_num <= 5

question: Show me the loan products with the highest average loan amount disbursed, considering only loans with a disbursed amount greater than the average disbursed amount of all loans, and rank them by their average interest rate
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans) GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rl.name, rl.avg_disbursed_amount, rl.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_loans rl WHERE rl.rank = 1

question: Find the borrowers who have applied for a loan with a requested amount greater than the average requested amount of all loans, and what is their average annual income, considering only borrowers with an annual income greater than the average annual income of all borrowers
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.annual_income, AVG(la.requested_amount) AS avg_requested_amount, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id ORDER BY b.annual_income DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id WHERE la.requested_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications) AND b.annual_income > (SELECT AVG(annual_income) FROM borrowers) GROUP BY b.borrower_id, b.full_name, b.annual_income) SELECT rb.full_name, rb.annual_income, rb.avg_requested_amount FROM ranked_borrowers rb WHERE rb.rank = 1

question: Which loan products have the highest average repayment amount, considering only loans with a repayment status of 'paid' and a payment mode of 'online', and what is their average interest rate
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(r.paid_amount) AS avg_repayment_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(r.paid_amount) DESC) AS ntile FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE r.status = 'paid' AND r.payment_mode = 'online' GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rl.name, rl.avg_repayment_amount, rl.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_loans rl WHERE rl.ntile = 1

question: Find the top 10 loan products with the highest average disbursed amount, considering only loans with a disbursed amount greater than the average disbursed amount of all loans, and rank them by their average interest rate
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.name, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans) GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.name) SELECT rl.name, rl.avg_disbursed_amount, rl.avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_loans rl WHERE rl.row_num <= 10

question: Which borrowers have the highest average loan amount per city, considering only loans that are not non-performing assets?
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, b.borrower_id, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_loan_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.npa_flag = FALSE GROUP BY b.city, b.borrower_id) SELECT city, borrower_id, avg_loan_amount FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num = 1

question: Find the top 3 loan products with the highest total disbursed amount for each employment type, considering only borrowers with a credit score above 600
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, b.employment_type, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.employment_type ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE b.credit_score > 600 GROUP BY lp.product_id, b.employment_type) SELECT product_id, employment_type, total_disbursed FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 3

question: List the borrowers with the highest total repayment amount for each city, considering only repayments made within the due date
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, b.borrower_id, SUM(r.paid_amount) AS total_repayment, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY SUM(r.paid_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE r.paid_date <= r.due_date GROUP BY b.city, b.borrower_id) SELECT city, borrower_id, total_repayment FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num = 1

question: What are the top 2 loan products with the lowest average interest rate for each product type, considering only loan applications with a status of 'approved'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.product_type, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_type ORDER BY AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) ASC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE la.status = 'approved' GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.product_type) SELECT product_id, product_type, avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 2

question: Find the borrowers with the highest total loan amount for each employment type, considering only loans that are not non-performing assets and have a credit score above 500
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.employment_type, b.borrower_id, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_loan, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.employment_type ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.npa_flag = FALSE AND b.credit_score > 500 GROUP BY b.employment_type, b.borrower_id) SELECT employment_type, borrower_id, total_loan FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num = 1

question: List the loan products with the highest total repayment amount for each product type, considering only repayments made within the due date and loan applications with a status of 'approved'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.product_type, SUM(r.paid_amount) AS total_repayment, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_type ORDER BY SUM(r.paid_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN repayments r ON l.loan_id = r.loan_id WHERE la.status = 'approved' AND r.paid_date <= r.due_date GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.product_type) SELECT product_id, product_type, total_repayment FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1

question: Find the top 3 loan products with the highest total disbursed amount for each employment type, considering only borrowers with a credit score above 700 and loan applications with a status of 'approved'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, b.employment_type, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.employment_type ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE b.credit_score > 700 AND la.status = 'approved' GROUP BY lp.product_id, b.employment_type) SELECT product_id, employment_type, total_disbursed FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 3

question: List the borrowers with the highest total loan amount for each city, considering only loans that are not non-performing assets and have a credit score above 600
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, b.borrower_id, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_loan, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.npa_flag = FALSE AND b.credit_score > 600 GROUP BY b.city, b.borrower_id) SELECT city, borrower_id, total_loan FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num = 1

question: What are the top 2 loan products with the lowest average interest rate for each product type, considering only loan applications with a status of 'approved' and a credit score above 500?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, lp.product_type, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_type ORDER BY AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) ASC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE la.status = 'approved' AND b.credit_score > 500 GROUP BY lp.product_id, lp.product_type) SELECT product_id, product_type, avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 2

question: List all borrowers with a credit score above 700 who have taken a loan with an interest rate above 15%, along with their loan details and the average interest rate of their loan product, ranked by the loan amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, l.loan_id, l.disbursed_amount, lp.interest_rate_pct, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id) AS avg_interest_rate, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE b.credit_score > 700 AND lp.interest_rate_pct > 15) SELECT borrower_id, full_name, loan_id, disbursed_amount, interest_rate_pct, avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE rank = 1

question: Find the total disbursed amount for each loan product, along with the number of borrowers who have taken that loan product, and rank the loan products by their total disbursed amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id) SELECT product_id, total_disbursed_amount, num_borrowers FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 5

question: What are the top 2 loan products with the highest average credit score of borrowers, considering only loan products with a minimum amount above 50000?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE lp.min_amount > 50000 GROUP BY lp.product_id) SELECT product_id, avg_credit_score FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 2

question: Find the total outstanding amount for each loan product, along with the number of loans that are non-performing assets, and rank the loan products by their total outstanding amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, SUM(l.outstanding) AS total_outstanding_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN l.npa_flag = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS num_npa_loans, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.outstanding) DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY lp.product_id) SELECT product_id, total_outstanding_amount, num_npa_loans FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 5

question: List all borrowers who have taken a loan with an interest rate above 12%, along with their loan details and the average interest rate of their loan product, ranked by the loan amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, b.full_name, l.loan_id, l.disbursed_amount, lp.interest_rate_pct, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id) AS avg_interest_rate, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.borrower_id ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE lp.interest_rate_pct > 12) SELECT borrower_id, full_name, loan_id, disbursed_amount, interest_rate_pct, avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE rank = 1

question: Find the total disbursed amount for each employment type, along with the number of borrowers who have taken a loan, and rank the employment types by their total disbursed amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_employment_types AS (SELECT b.employment_type, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT b.borrower_id) AS num_borrowers, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id GROUP BY b.employment_type) SELECT employment_type, total_disbursed_amount, num_borrowers FROM ranked_employment_types WHERE rank <= 5

question: What are the top 2 cities with the highest average credit score of borrowers, considering only borrowers who have taken a loan with a minimum amount above 200000?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE lp.min_amount > 200000 GROUP BY b.city) SELECT city, avg_credit_score FROM ranked_cities WHERE row_num <= 2

question: Which borrowers have the highest total loan amount disbursed in the last quarter, grouped by employment type and ranked by total disbursed amount?
sql: WITH recent_loans AS (SELECT l.disbursed_amount, b.employment_type, b.borrower_id FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE l.disbursed_at >= (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 quarter')), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT employment_type, borrower_id, SUM(disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY employment_type ORDER BY SUM(disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM recent_loans GROUP BY employment_type, borrower_id) SELECT employment_type, borrower_id, total_disbursed FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE rank = 1

question: What are the bottom 20% of loan products by average credit score of borrowers, considering only approved loan applications?
sql: WITH approved_applications AS (SELECT la.product_id, b.credit_score FROM loan_applications la JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE la.status = 'approved'), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, AVG(credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(credit_score)) AS ntile FROM approved_applications GROUP BY product_id) SELECT product_id, avg_credit_score FROM ranked_products WHERE ntile = 1

question: List all borrowers who have at least two active loans with a current outstanding amount greater than the average outstanding amount for their employment type, ranked by the total outstanding amount.
sql: WITH active_loans AS (SELECT l.loan_id, l.outstanding, b.employment_type, b.borrower_id FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE l.outstanding > 0), avg_outstanding AS (SELECT employment_type, AVG(outstanding) AS avg_outstanding FROM active_loans GROUP BY employment_type), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT employment_type, borrower_id, SUM(outstanding) AS total_outstanding, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY employment_type ORDER BY SUM(outstanding) DESC) AS rank FROM active_loans WHERE (borrower_id, employment_type) IN (SELECT borrower_id, employment_type FROM active_loans WHERE outstanding > (SELECT avg_outstanding FROM avg_outstanding WHERE employment_type = active_loans.employment_type) GROUP BY borrower_id, employment_type HAVING COUNT(loan_id) >= 2) GROUP BY employment_type, borrower_id) SELECT employment_type, borrower_id, total_outstanding FROM ranked_borrowers

question: What is the average disbursed amount per borrower for the top 10% of loan products by average disbursed amount, considering only loans with no repayment overdue?
sql: WITH repaid_loans AS (SELECT l.loan_id, l.disbursed_amount, lp.product_id FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE l.outstanding = 0 AND l.loan_id NOT IN (SELECT loan_id FROM repayments WHERE due_date < paid_date)), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, AVG(disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(disbursed_amount) DESC) AS ntile FROM repaid_loans GROUP BY product_id) SELECT AVG(avg_disbursed) AS avg_disbursed FROM ranked_products WHERE ntile = 1

question: What are the top 5 loan products by total disbursed amount for borrowers with a credit score above the average credit score for their employment type?
sql: WITH avg_credit_score AS (SELECT employment_type, AVG(credit_score) AS avg_credit_score FROM borrowers GROUP BY employment_type), eligible_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, employment_type FROM borrowers WHERE credit_score > (SELECT avg_credit_score FROM avg_credit_score WHERE employment_type = borrowers.employment_type)), ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) AS total_disbursed, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN eligible_borrowers eb ON la.borrower_id = eb.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id GROUP BY lp.product_id) SELECT product_id, total_disbursed FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 5

question: List all borrowers who have at least one loan with a current outstanding amount greater than the average outstanding amount for their city, ranked by the total outstanding amount.
sql: WITH avg_outstanding AS (SELECT city, AVG(outstanding) AS avg_outstanding FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id GROUP BY city), ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, b.borrower_id, SUM(l.outstanding) AS total_outstanding, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY SUM(l.outstanding) DESC) AS rank FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE l.outstanding > (SELECT avg_outstanding FROM avg_outstanding WHERE city = b.city) GROUP BY b.city, b.borrower_id) SELECT city, borrower_id, total_outstanding FROM ranked_borrowers

question: Which loan products have the highest average repayment amount per loan, considering only loans with all repayments made on time?
sql: WITH repaid_loans AS (SELECT l.loan_id, l.application_id, lp.product_id FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE l.outstanding = 0 AND l.loan_id NOT IN (SELECT loan_id FROM repayments WHERE due_date > paid_date)), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, AVG(r.paid_amount) AS avg_repayment, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(r.paid_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM repaid_loans rl JOIN repayments r ON rl.loan_id = r.loan_id GROUP BY product_id) SELECT product_id, avg_repayment FROM ranked_products WHERE rank = 1

question: Which borrowers have the highest average credit score per city, considering only borrowers with a credit score above the overall average, and rank them by their credit score?
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.full_name, b.city, b.credit_score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY b.credit_score DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b WHERE b.credit_score > (SELECT AVG(credit_score) FROM borrowers)) SELECT rb.city, AVG(rb.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, rb.full_name FROM ranked_borrowers rb GROUP BY rb.city, rb.full_name ORDER BY avg_credit_score DESC

question: What are the borrowers with the highest number of loan applications per city, considering only borrowers with more than one loan application, and rank them by the number of applications?
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.full_name, b.city, COUNT(la.application_id) AS num_applications, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY COUNT(la.application_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.full_name, b.city HAVING COUNT(la.application_id) > 1) SELECT rb.city, rb.full_name, rb.num_applications FROM ranked_borrowers rb ORDER BY rb.num_applications DESC

question: Find the loan products with the lowest average interest rate per product type, considering only products with an interest rate below the overall average, and calculate the moving average of interest rates
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_type, lp.interest_rate_pct, AVG(lp.interest_rate_pct) OVER (ORDER BY lp.interest_rate_pct) AS moving_avg, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_type ORDER BY lp.interest_rate_pct) AS row_num FROM loan_products lp WHERE lp.interest_rate_pct < (SELECT AVG(interest_rate_pct) FROM loan_products)) SELECT rp.product_type, AVG(rp.interest_rate_pct) AS avg_interest_rate FROM ranked_products rp GROUP BY rp.product_type ORDER BY avg_interest_rate ASC

question: Which cities have the highest average annual income per borrower, considering only borrowers with an annual income above the overall average, and calculate the percentile of annual incomes
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.city, b.annual_income, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY b.annual_income) AS percentile, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY b.annual_income DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b WHERE b.annual_income > (SELECT AVG(annual_income) FROM borrowers)) SELECT rb.city, AVG(rb.annual_income) AS avg_annual_income FROM ranked_borrowers rb GROUP BY rb.city ORDER BY avg_annual_income DESC

question: Show me the top 5 loan applications with the highest requested amount per borrower, considering only applications with a requested amount above the overall average, and rank them by the requested amount
sql: WITH ranked_applications AS (SELECT la.borrower_id, la.requested_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY la.borrower_id ORDER BY la.requested_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la WHERE la.requested_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications)) SELECT ra.borrower_id, ra.requested_amount FROM ranked_applications ra ORDER BY ra.requested_amount DESC LIMIT 5

question: What are the loan products with the highest average disbursed amount per product type, considering only products with a disbursed amount above the overall average, and calculate the running total of disbursed amounts
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_type, l.disbursed_amount, SUM(l.disbursed_amount) OVER (ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount) AS running_total, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lp.product_type ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans)) SELECT rp.product_type, AVG(rp.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_products rp GROUP BY rp.product_type ORDER BY avg_disbursed_amount DESC

question: Find the borrowers who have taken a loan with a disbursed amount above the average disbursed amount per city, and rank them by the disbursed amount
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT b.full_name, l.disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans)) SELECT rl.full_name, rl.disbursed_amount FROM ranked_loans rl ORDER BY rl.disbursed_amount DESC

question: List all loan applications with a requested amount above the average requested amount per product, and rank them by the requested amount
sql: WITH ranked_applications AS (SELECT la.application_id, la.requested_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY la.product_id ORDER BY la.requested_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la WHERE la.requested_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications)) SELECT ra.application_id, ra.requested_amount FROM ranked_applications ra ORDER BY ra.requested_amount DESC

question: What are the top 3 loan products with the highest average disbursed amount per borrower, considering only loans with a disbursed amount greater than the average requested amount across all loan applications, and rank them by their average disbursed amount in descending order?
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT la.product_id, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY la.product_id ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE l.disbursed_amount > (SELECT AVG(requested_amount) FROM loan_applications) GROUP BY la.product_id) SELECT lp.name, rl.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_loans rl JOIN loan_products lp ON rl.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE rl.row_num <= 3

question: What are the top 2 loan applications with the highest disbursed amount per borrower, considering only borrowers who have taken out loans with an interest rate greater than 12%, and rank them by their disbursed amount in descending order, using a subquery to filter out borrowers with no loans
sql: WITH ranked_applications AS (SELECT la.application_id, l.disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY la.borrower_id ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE lp.interest_rate_pct > 12 AND la.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications)) SELECT ra.application_id, ra.disbursed_amount FROM ranked_applications ra WHERE ra.row_num <= 2

question: Find the loan products with an average disbursed amount per borrower less than the overall average disbursed amount across all loan products, and rank them by their average disbursed amount in ascending order, using a subquery to filter out products with no loans
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed_amount, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) ASC) AS rank FROM loan_products lp JOIN loan_applications la ON lp.product_id = la.product_id JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id WHERE lp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM loan_applications) GROUP BY lp.product_id) SELECT rp.product_id, rp.avg_disbursed_amount FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.avg_disbursed_amount < (SELECT AVG(disbursed_amount) FROM loans)

question: What are the top 3 loan applications with the highest disbursed amount per borrower, considering only borrowers who have taken out loans with an interest rate less than 10%, and rank them by their disbursed amount in descending order, using a subquery to filter out borrowers with no loans
sql: WITH ranked_applications AS (SELECT la.application_id, l.disbursed_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY la.borrower_id ORDER BY l.disbursed_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE lp.interest_rate_pct < 10 AND la.borrower_id IN (SELECT borrower_id FROM loan_applications)) SELECT ra.application_id, ra.disbursed_amount FROM ranked_applications ra WHERE ra.row_num <= 3

question: What are the top 3 loan products by average disbursed amount per borrower city, considering only fully repaid loans?
sql: WITH repaid_loans AS (SELECT la.borrower_id, lp.product_id, l.disbursed_amount, b.city FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE l.outstanding = 0), ranked_products AS (SELECT city, product_id, AVG(disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY city ORDER BY AVG(disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM repaid_loans GROUP BY city, product_id) SELECT city, product_id, avg_disbursed FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 3

question: List the loan products with the highest interest rate and their corresponding average disbursed amount per borrower employment type, considering only loans with a tenure of more than 12 months
sql: WITH filtered_loans AS (SELECT la.product_id, b.employment_type, l.disbursed_amount FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE la.requested_tenure > 12), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, employment_type, AVG(disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY employment_type ORDER BY AVG(disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM filtered_loans GROUP BY product_id, employment_type) SELECT lp.name, lp.interest_rate_pct, rp.avg_disbursed FROM ranked_products rp JOIN loan_products lp ON rp.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE rp.rank = 1 AND lp.interest_rate_pct = (SELECT MAX(interest_rate_pct) FROM loan_products)

question: What is the total outstanding amount for loans with a DPD of more than 30 days, grouped by borrower city and loan product type, and ranked by the total outstanding amount?
sql: WITH ranked_loans AS (SELECT b.city, lp.product_type, SUM(l.outstanding) AS total_outstanding, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b.city ORDER BY SUM(l.outstanding) DESC) AS row_num FROM loans l JOIN loan_applications la ON l.application_id = la.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE l.dpd > 30 GROUP BY b.city, lp.product_type) SELECT city, product_type, total_outstanding FROM ranked_loans

question: Find the borrowers with the highest average credit score among their loan applications, and their corresponding total requested amount, considering only loan applications with a status of 'approved'
sql: WITH ranked_borrowers AS (SELECT b.borrower_id, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit, SUM(la.requested_amount) AS total_requested, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id WHERE la.status = 'approved' GROUP BY b.borrower_id), top_borrowers AS (SELECT borrower_id, avg_credit, total_requested FROM ranked_borrowers WHERE row_num = 1) SELECT * FROM top_borrowers

question: List the loan products with the lowest interest rate and their corresponding average disbursed amount per borrower employment type, considering only loans with a tenure of less than or equal to 12 months
sql: WITH filtered_loans AS (SELECT la.product_id, b.employment_type, l.disbursed_amount FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN borrowers b ON la.borrower_id = b.borrower_id WHERE la.requested_tenure <= 12), ranked_products AS (SELECT product_id, employment_type, AVG(disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY employment_type ORDER BY AVG(disbursed_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM filtered_loans GROUP BY product_id, employment_type) SELECT lp.name, lp.interest_rate_pct, rp.avg_disbursed FROM ranked_products rp JOIN loan_products lp ON rp.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE rp.rank = 1 AND lp.interest_rate_pct = (SELECT MIN(interest_rate_pct) FROM loan_products)

question: What are the top 3 borrower cities by average credit score, considering only borrowers with at least one loan application?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT b.city, AVG(b.credit_score) AS avg_credit, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(b.credit_score) DESC) AS row_num FROM borrowers b JOIN loan_applications la ON b.borrower_id = la.borrower_id GROUP BY b.city) SELECT city, avg_credit FROM ranked_cities WHERE row_num <= 3

question: Find the loan products with the highest average disbursed amount and their corresponding total requested amount, considering only loan applications with a status of 'approved' and a requested tenure of more than 12 months
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT lp.product_id, AVG(l.disbursed_amount) AS avg_disbursed, SUM(la.requested_amount) AS total_requested, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(l.disbursed_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM loan_applications la JOIN loans l ON la.application_id = l.application_id JOIN loan_products lp ON la.product_id = lp.product_id WHERE la.status = 'approved' AND la.requested_tenure > 12 GROUP BY lp.product_id), top_products AS (SELECT product_id, avg_disbursed, total_requested FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1) SELECT * FROM top_products

question: What are the top 3 policies for each product by sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.policy_id, ip.name AS product_name, p.sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ip.product_id ORDER BY p.sum_insured DESC) AS row_num FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id) SELECT product_name, policy_id, sum_insured FROM ranked_policies WHERE row_num <= 3

question: List all policyholders with more than one policy, along with the average premium of their policies
sql: SELECT ph.full_name, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE ph.holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies GROUP BY holder_id HAVING COUNT(policy_id) > 1) GROUP BY ph.full_name

question: Find the total claims amount for each region, along with the rank of the region by total claims amount
sql: WITH region_claims AS (SELECT a.region, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claims FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN agents a ON p.holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE city = a.region) GROUP BY a.region) SELECT region, total_claims, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_claims DESC) AS rank FROM region_claims

question: Which agents have more than 50% of their policies with a status of 'active'?
sql: WITH agent_policies AS (SELECT a.agent_id, COUNT(CASE WHEN p.status = 'active' THEN 1 END) AS active_policies, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE city = a.region) GROUP BY a.agent_id) SELECT agent_id FROM agent_policies WHERE active_policies * 1.0 / total_policies > 0.5 AND agent_id IN (SELECT agent_id FROM agents WHERE active_policies > 0)

question: What is the average sum insured for policies in each city, along with the top 2 policies by sum insured in each city?
sql: WITH city_policies AS (SELECT ph.city, p.policy_id, p.sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY p.sum_insured DESC) AS row_num FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id) SELECT city, AVG(sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, policy_id FROM city_policies WHERE row_num <= 2 GROUP BY city, policy_id

question: Find the total premium for each product, along with the percentage of total premium for each product
sql: WITH product_premiums AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name AS product_name, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name) SELECT product_name, total_premium, total_premium * 1.0 / (SELECT SUM(total_premium) FROM product_premiums) AS percentage FROM product_premiums

question: List all policyholders who have filed more than one claim, along with the average claim amount
sql: SELECT ph.full_name, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id WHERE ph.holder_id IN (SELECT p.holder_id FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id GROUP BY p.holder_id HAVING COUNT(c.claim_id) > 1) GROUP BY ph.full_name

question: What is the total sum insured for each occupation, along with the rank of the occupation by total sum insured?
sql: WITH occupation_sum_insured AS (SELECT ph.occupation, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.occupation) SELECT occupation, total_sum_insured, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_sum_insured DESC) AS rank FROM occupation_sum_insured

question: Find the top 2 agents by total policies in each region, along with the total premium of their policies
sql: WITH agent_policies AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.region, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a.region ORDER BY COUNT(p.policy_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE city = a.region) GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.region) SELECT region, agent_id, total_policies, total_premium FROM agent_policies WHERE row_num <= 2

question: What is the average claim amount for each claim type, along with the top 2 claims by amount in each claim type?
sql: WITH claim_amounts AS (SELECT c.claim_type, c.claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.claim_type ORDER BY c.claim_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM claims c) SELECT claim_type, AVG(claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, claim_amount FROM claim_amounts WHERE row_num <= 2 GROUP BY claim_type, claim_amount

question: What are the top 3 cities with the highest average sum insured, along with their rank and the total number of policies?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT city, AVG(sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, COUNT(policy_id) AS total_policies, RANK() OVER(ORDER BY AVG(sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, avg_sum_insured, total_policies, rank FROM ranked_cities WHERE rank <= 3

question: List all the policyholders who have purchased more than one policy, along with the total number of policies and the average sum insured for each policyholder
sql: WITH policy_counts AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) > 1) SELECT holder_id, full_name, total_policies, avg_sum_insured FROM policy_counts ORDER BY total_policies DESC

question: Find the top 10% of agents with the highest commission percentage, along with their total active policies and commission percentage
sql: WITH agent_commissions AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.commission_pct, a.active_policies, NTILE(10) OVER(ORDER BY a.commission_pct DESC) AS percentile FROM agents a) SELECT agent_id, name, commission_pct, active_policies FROM agent_commissions WHERE percentile = 1

question: What are the policies with the highest premium annual in each city, along with the policy details and the city's average premium annual?
sql: WITH city_premium_ranks AS (SELECT p.policy_id, p.sum_insured, p.premium_annual, ph.city, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY p.premium_annual DESC) AS rank, AVG(p.premium_annual) OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city) AS avg_premium_annual FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id) SELECT policy_id, sum_insured, premium_annual, city, avg_premium_annual FROM city_premium_ranks WHERE rank = 1

question: List all the insurance products with more than 100 policies sold, along with the total number of policies and the average sum insured for each product
sql: WITH product_sales AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) > 100) SELECT product_id, name, total_policies, avg_sum_insured FROM product_sales ORDER BY total_policies DESC

question: Find the policies with the highest sum insured in each city, along with the policy details and the city's average sum insured
sql: WITH city_sum_insured_ranks AS (SELECT p.policy_id, p.sum_insured, p.premium_annual, ph.city, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY p.sum_insured DESC) AS rank, AVG(p.sum_insured) OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city) AS avg_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id) SELECT policy_id, sum_insured, premium_annual, city, avg_sum_insured FROM city_sum_insured_ranks WHERE rank = 1

question: What are the agents with the highest commission percentage in each region, along with their total active policies and commission percentage?
sql: WITH region_agent_commissions AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.commission_pct, a.active_policies, a.region, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY a.region ORDER BY a.commission_pct DESC) AS rank FROM agents a) SELECT agent_id, name, commission_pct, active_policies, region FROM region_agent_commissions WHERE rank = 1

question: Show me the policyholders who have purchased policies with a sum insured greater than the average sum insured for their city, along with the policy details and the city's average sum insured
sql: WITH city_avg_sum_insured AS (SELECT ph.city, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city), policyholders_with_high_sum_insured AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, p.policy_id, p.sum_insured, p.premium_annual, ph.city FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE p.sum_insured > (SELECT avg_sum_insured FROM city_avg_sum_insured WHERE city = ph.city)) SELECT holder_id, full_name, policy_id, sum_insured, premium_annual, city FROM policyholders_with_high_sum_insured

question: What are the top 3 policies with the highest sum insured for each insurance product, ranked by premium?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.policy_id, ip.name, p.sum_insured, p.premium_annual, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ip.product_id ORDER BY p.sum_insured DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id) SELECT name, policy_id, sum_insured, premium_annual FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank <= 3 GROUP BY name, policy_id, sum_insured, premium_annual ORDER BY name, sum_insured DESC

question: List the total claims amount for each policyholder, along with their rank in claims amount within their city?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.city, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claims, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.city) SELECT holder_id, city, total_claims, rank FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE total_claims > (SELECT AVG(total_claims) FROM (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.city, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claims FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.city) AS subquery)

question: What are the top 3 highest sum insured policies for each city, along with the average premium and total claims amount?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.policy_id, ph.city, p.sum_insured, p.premium_annual, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY p.sum_insured DESC) AS row_num, (SELECT SUM(c.claim_amount) FROM claims c WHERE c.policy_id = p.policy_id) AS total_claims FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id) SELECT city, sum_insured, premium_annual, total_claims FROM ranked_policies WHERE row_num <= 3 GROUP BY city, sum_insured, premium_annual, total_claims ORDER BY city, sum_insured DESC

question: Find the policies with the highest average claim amount per policyholder, grouped by occupation and product type?
sql: WITH avg_claim_amount AS (SELECT ph.occupation, ip.type, AVG(c.claim_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY ph.occupation, ip.type) AS avg_claim FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT occupation, type, avg_claim, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation ORDER BY avg_claim DESC) AS rank FROM avg_claim_amount) SELECT occupation, type, avg_claim FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank = 1 GROUP BY occupation, type, avg_claim

question: List the top 5 agents by total policies sold, along with their commission percentage and the average sum insured of their policies?
sql: WITH agent_policies AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.commission_pct, p.sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(p.policy_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name, a.commission_pct, p.sum_insured), ranked_agents AS (SELECT agent_id, name, commission_pct, sum_insured, row_num, NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY row_num) AS ntile FROM agent_policies) SELECT agent_id, name, commission_pct, AVG(sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM ranked_agents WHERE ntile = 1 GROUP BY agent_id, name, commission_pct ORDER BY avg_sum_insured DESC

question: What are the policies with the highest claim amount, grouped by product type and city, and ranked by their claim amount?
sql: WITH claim_amounts AS (SELECT ip.type, ph.city, c.claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ip.type, ph.city ORDER BY c.claim_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT type, city, claim_amount, row_num, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY claim_amount DESC) AS rank FROM claim_amounts) SELECT type, city, claim_amount FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank = 1 GROUP BY type, city, claim_amount ORDER BY type, city

question: Find the average premium and sum insured of policies, grouped by occupation and product type, and calculate the moving average of the sum insured?
sql: WITH policy_data AS (SELECT ph.occupation, ip.type, p.premium_annual, p.sum_insured, AVG(p.sum_insured) OVER (PARTITION BY ph.occupation, ip.type ORDER BY p.premium_annual) AS moving_avg FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT occupation, type, premium_annual, sum_insured, moving_avg, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation, type ORDER BY premium_annual) AS row_num FROM policy_data) SELECT occupation, type, AVG(premium_annual) AS avg_premium, AVG(sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, AVG(moving_avg) AS avg_moving_avg FROM ranked_policies GROUP BY occupation, type ORDER BY occupation, type

question: List the policies with the highest claim amount, grouped by product type and agent, and ranked by their claim amount?
sql: WITH claim_amounts AS (SELECT ip.type, a.name, c.claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ip.type, a.name ORDER BY c.claim_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id JOIN agents a ON ph.holder_id = a.agent_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT type, name, claim_amount, row_num, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY claim_amount DESC) AS rank FROM claim_amounts) SELECT type, name, claim_amount FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank = 1 GROUP BY type, name, claim_amount ORDER BY type, name

question: What are the policies with the highest premium, grouped by occupation and product type, and calculate the running total of the premium?
sql: WITH policy_data AS (SELECT ph.occupation, ip.type, p.premium_annual, SUM(p.premium_annual) OVER (PARTITION BY ph.occupation, ip.type ORDER BY p.premium_annual) AS running_total FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT occupation, type, premium_annual, running_total, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation, type ORDER BY premium_annual DESC) AS row_num FROM policy_data) SELECT occupation, type, AVG(premium_annual) AS avg_premium, AVG(running_total) AS avg_running_total FROM ranked_policies GROUP BY occupation, type ORDER BY occupation, type

question: Find the agents with the highest total policies sold, grouped by region and product type, and calculate the average commission percentage?
sql: WITH agent_policies AS (SELECT a.region, ip.type, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies, AVG(a.commission_pct) AS avg_commission FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY a.region, ip.type), ranked_agents AS (SELECT region, type, total_policies, avg_commission, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY region, type ORDER BY total_policies DESC) AS row_num FROM agent_policies) SELECT region, type, AVG(total_policies) AS avg_total_policies, AVG(avg_commission) AS avg_avg_commission FROM ranked_agents GROUP BY region, type ORDER BY region, type

question: List the policies with the highest claim amount, grouped by city and product type, and ranked by their claim amount?
sql: WITH claim_amounts AS (SELECT ph.city, ip.type, c.claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ph.city, ip.type ORDER BY c.claim_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT city, type, claim_amount, row_num, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY city, type ORDER BY claim_amount DESC) AS rank FROM claim_amounts) SELECT city, type, claim_amount FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank = 1 GROUP BY city, type, claim_amount ORDER BY city, type

question: What are the policies with the highest premium, grouped by occupation and agent, and calculate the moving average of the premium?
sql: WITH policy_data AS (SELECT ph.occupation, a.name, p.premium_annual, AVG(p.premium_annual) OVER (PARTITION BY ph.occupation, a.name ORDER BY p.premium_annual) AS moving_avg FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN agents a ON ph.holder_id = a.agent_id), ranked_policies AS (SELECT occupation, name, premium_annual, moving_avg, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation, name ORDER BY premium_annual) AS row_num FROM policy_data) SELECT occupation, name, AVG(premium_annual) AS avg_premium, AVG(moving_avg) AS avg_moving_avg FROM ranked_policies GROUP BY occupation, name ORDER BY occupation, name

question: Which policyholders have the highest total sum insured in each city, considering only active policies?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS row_num FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city) SELECT * FROM ranked_policies WHERE row_num = 1

question: List the top 3 agents in each region based on the average premium of their policies, excluding agents with less than 5 active policies
sql: WITH agent_premiums AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name, a.region HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) >= 5), ranked_agents AS (SELECT *, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY avg_premium DESC) AS rank FROM agent_premiums) SELECT * FROM ranked_agents WHERE rank <= 3

question: Find the policyholders with the highest claim amount in each occupation, considering only claims with a status of 'settled'
sql: WITH ranked_claims AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.occupation, c.claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.occupation ORDER BY c.claim_amount DESC) AS row_num FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id WHERE c.status = 'settled') SELECT * FROM ranked_claims WHERE row_num = 1

question: Show me the agents with the highest total commission in each region, considering only policies with a premium above the average premium of the corresponding insurance product
sql: WITH product_avg_premium AS (SELECT product_id, AVG(premium_annual) AS avg_premium FROM policies GROUP BY product_id), agent_commissions AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, SUM(p.premium_annual * a.commission_pct) AS total_commission FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id JOIN product_avg_premium pap ON p.product_id = pap.product_id WHERE p.premium_annual > pap.avg_premium GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name, a.region), ranked_agents AS (SELECT *, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_commission DESC) AS rank FROM agent_commissions) SELECT * FROM ranked_agents WHERE rank = 1

question: Which insurance products have the highest average sum insured in each type, considering only policies with a status of 'active'?
sql: WITH product_avg_sum_insured AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type), ranked_products AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY type ORDER BY avg_sum_insured DESC) AS row_num FROM product_avg_sum_insured) SELECT * FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num = 1
