question: Find the average trade size for each trade type, considering only trades made in the last year, and rank them by their average trade size?
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT t.trade_type, t.quantity, t.traded_at FROM trades t WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 year'), ranked_trades AS (SELECT rt.trade_type, AVG(rt.quantity) AS avg_trade_size, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY AVG(rt.quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM recent_trades rt GROUP BY rt.trade_type) SELECT rt.trade_type, rt.avg_trade_size FROM ranked_trades rt WHERE rt.row_num <= 5 AND rt.trade_type IN (SELECT trade_type FROM trades WHERE status = 'completed') ORDER BY rt.avg_trade_size DESC

question: What are the top 5 instruments by total holding value, considering only portfolios created in the last 6 months, and grouped by their sector?
sql: WITH recent_portfolios AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, p.name, p.created_at FROM portfolios p WHERE p.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '6 month'), ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.sector, i.instrument_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_holding_value, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY i.sector ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM recent_portfolios rp JOIN holdings h ON rp.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id GROUP BY i.sector, i.instrument_id) SELECT ri.sector, ri.instrument_id, ri.total_holding_value FROM ranked_instruments ri WHERE ri.rank <= 5 AND ri.sector IN (SELECT sector FROM instruments WHERE market_cap_cr > (SELECT AVG(market_cap_cr) FROM instruments)) ORDER BY ri.total_holding_value DESC

question: Which are the top 3 portfolios by total value for each investor, considering only the portfolios with more than one holding?
sql: WITH ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY p.investor_id ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id HAVING COUNT(h.holding_id) > 1) SELECT investor_id, portfolio_id, total_value FROM ranked_portfolios WHERE row_num <= 3

question: Find the average brokerage per trade type for the top 20% of investors by total traded value, using a subquery to calculate the total traded value for each investor
sql: WITH investor_traded_value AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(t.quantity * t.price) AS total_traded_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, total_traded_value, NTILE(5) OVER(ORDER BY total_traded_value DESC) AS ntile FROM investor_traded_value) SELECT t.trade_type, AVG(t.brokerage) AS avg_brokerage FROM trades t JOIN portfolios p ON t.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id JOIN investors i ON p.investor_id = i.investor_id WHERE i.investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM ranked_investors WHERE ntile = 1) GROUP BY t.trade_type

question: List all instruments with their average daily traded value over the last 30 days, using a window function to calculate the moving average and a subquery to filter out instruments with no trades
sql: WITH daily_traded_value AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE AS trade_date, SUM(t.quantity * t.price) AS total_traded_value FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE), moving_average AS (SELECT instrument_id, trade_date, total_traded_value, AVG(total_traded_value) OVER(PARTITION BY instrument_id ORDER BY trade_date ROWS 5 PRECEDING) AS moving_avg FROM daily_traded_value) SELECT i.name, i.symbol, ma.moving_avg FROM instruments i JOIN moving_average ma ON i.instrument_id = ma.instrument_id WHERE i.instrument_id IN (SELECT instrument_id FROM trades WHERE traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days')

question: What is the total market capitalization of the top 10% of instruments by market capitalization, using a subquery to calculate the market capitalization percentile and a window function to rank the instruments
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.market_cap_cr, NTILE(10) OVER(ORDER BY i.market_cap_cr DESC) AS ntile FROM instruments i) SELECT SUM(i.market_cap_cr) AS total_market_cap FROM instruments i WHERE i.instrument_id IN (SELECT instrument_id FROM ranked_instruments WHERE ntile = 1)

question: Find the investor with the highest total traded value for each trade type, using a subquery to calculate the total traded value for each investor and trade type, and a window function to rank the investors
sql: WITH investor_traded_value AS (SELECT i.investor_id, t.trade_type, SUM(t.quantity * t.price) AS total_traded_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id, t.trade_type), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, trade_type, total_traded_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY trade_type ORDER BY total_traded_value DESC) AS row_num FROM investor_traded_value) SELECT i.full_name, ri.trade_type, ri.total_traded_value FROM investors i JOIN ranked_investors ri ON i.investor_id = ri.investor_id WHERE ri.row_num = 1

question: List all portfolios with their total value and the rank of the portfolio within the investor, using a subquery to calculate the total value of each portfolio and a window function to rank the portfolios
sql: WITH portfolio_value AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, p.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id, p.investor_id), ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT investor_id, portfolio_id, total_value, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY investor_id ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS rank FROM portfolio_value) SELECT rp.investor_id, rp.portfolio_id, rp.total_value, rp.rank FROM ranked_portfolios rp

question: Which are the top 5 instruments by average daily traded value over the last 30 days, using a subquery to calculate the average daily traded value and a window function to rank the instruments
sql: WITH daily_traded_value AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE AS trade_date, SUM(t.quantity * t.price) AS total_traded_value FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE), avg_daily_traded_value AS (SELECT instrument_id, AVG(total_traded_value) AS avg_daily_traded_value FROM daily_traded_value GROUP BY instrument_id), ranked_instruments AS (SELECT instrument_id, avg_daily_traded_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY avg_daily_traded_value DESC) AS row_num FROM avg_daily_traded_value) SELECT i.name, i.symbol, ri.avg_daily_traded_value FROM instruments i JOIN ranked_instruments ri ON i.instrument_id = ri.instrument_id WHERE ri.row_num <= 5

question: Find the average quantity traded per day for each instrument, using a subquery to calculate the total quantity traded and a window function to calculate the moving average
sql: WITH daily_traded_quantity AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE AS trade_date, SUM(t.quantity) AS total_traded_quantity FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id GROUP BY i.instrument_id, t.traded_at::DATE), moving_average AS (SELECT instrument_id, trade_date, total_traded_quantity, AVG(total_traded_quantity) OVER(PARTITION BY instrument_id ORDER BY trade_date ROWS 5 PRECEDING) AS moving_avg FROM daily_traded_quantity) SELECT i.name, i.symbol, ma.moving_avg FROM instruments i JOIN moving_average ma ON i.instrument_id = ma.instrument_id

question: What is the total value of the top 20% of portfolios by total value, using a subquery to calculate the total value of each portfolio and a window function to rank the portfolios
sql: WITH portfolio_value AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, p.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id, p.investor_id), ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT investor_id, portfolio_id, total_value, NTILE(5) OVER(ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS ntile FROM portfolio_value) SELECT SUM(rp.total_value) AS total_value FROM ranked_portfolios rp WHERE rp.ntile = 1

question: List all investors with their total traded value and the rank of the investor within their risk profile, using a subquery to calculate the total traded value for each investor and a window function to rank the investors
sql: WITH investor_traded_value AS (SELECT i.investor_id, i.risk_profile, SUM(t.quantity * t.price) AS total_traded_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id, i.risk_profile), ranked_investors AS (SELECT risk_profile, investor_id, total_traded_value, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY risk_profile ORDER BY total_traded_value DESC) AS rank FROM investor_traded_value) SELECT ri.investor_id, i.full_name, ri.total_traded_value, ri.rank FROM ranked_investors ri JOIN investors i ON ri.investor_id = i.investor_id

question: What is the top 3 instrument by market cap in each sector?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT sector, symbol, market_cap_cr, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY sector ORDER BY market_cap_cr DESC) AS row_num FROM instruments) SELECT sector, symbol, market_cap_cr FROM ranked_instruments WHERE row_num <= 3

question: Find the total quantity of each instrument in portfolios, ranked by average holding value per investor
sql: WITH holdings_value AS (SELECT p.investor_id, i.symbol, SUM(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) AS total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY p.investor_id ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, i.symbol) SELECT investor_id, symbol, total_value FROM holdings_value WHERE row_num = 1

question: List all investors with more than one portfolio, ordered by the total number of portfolios per investor
sql: WITH investor_portfolios AS (SELECT i.investor_id, COUNT(p.portfolio_id) AS num_portfolios, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY COUNT(p.portfolio_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id GROUP BY i.investor_id HAVING COUNT(p.portfolio_id) > 1) SELECT investor_id, num_portfolios FROM investor_portfolios

question: What is the moving average of trade prices for each instrument over the last 30 days?
sql: WITH trade_prices AS (SELECT i.symbol, t.traded_at, t.price, AVG(t.price) OVER(PARTITION BY i.symbol ORDER BY t.traded_at ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS moving_avg FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY) SELECT symbol, traded_at, price, moving_avg FROM trade_prices

question: Find the top 10% of investors by total investment value, using a subquery to calculate the threshold
sql: WITH investor_investments AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) AS total_investment, NTILE(10) OVER(ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) DESC) AS percentile FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id) SELECT investor_id, total_investment FROM investor_investments WHERE percentile = 1 AND total_investment > (SELECT AVG(total_investment) FROM investor_investments)

question: Which instruments have the highest average trade quantity in each sector?
sql: WITH sector_quantities AS (SELECT i.sector, i.symbol, AVG(t.quantity) AS avg_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY i.sector ORDER BY AVG(t.quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id GROUP BY i.sector, i.symbol) SELECT sector, symbol, avg_quantity FROM sector_quantities WHERE row_num = 1

question: Find the total market cap of all instruments in each portfolio, ranked by the total market cap per portfolio
sql: WITH portfolio_market_cap AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, SUM(i.market_cap_cr) AS total_market_cap, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(i.market_cap_cr) DESC) AS row_num FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id) SELECT portfolio_id, total_market_cap FROM portfolio_market_cap

question: What is the running total of trade quantities for each instrument over the last 30 days?
sql: WITH trade_quantities AS (SELECT i.symbol, t.traded_at, t.quantity, SUM(t.quantity) OVER(PARTITION BY i.symbol ORDER BY t.traded_at ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_total FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY) SELECT symbol, traded_at, quantity, running_total FROM trade_quantities

question: Find the average holding value per investor, using a subquery to calculate the total holding value per portfolio
sql: WITH portfolio_holdings AS (SELECT p.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) AS total_holding_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id) SELECT i.investor_id, AVG(ph.total_holding_value) AS avg_holding_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolio_holdings ph ON i.investor_id = ph.investor_id GROUP BY i.investor_id

question: What are the top 5 sectors by average market capitalization, considering only instruments with a market capitalization greater than the average market capitalization of all instruments?
sql: WITH ranked_sectors AS (SELECT i.sector, AVG(i.market_cap_cr) AS avg_market_cap, DENSE_RANK() OVER(ORDER BY AVG(i.market_cap_cr) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i WHERE i.market_cap_cr > (SELECT AVG(market_cap_cr) FROM instruments) GROUP BY i.sector) SELECT sector, avg_market_cap FROM ranked_sectors WHERE rank <= 5

question: List all investors with their total holdings value, and rank them by their holdings value as a percentage of their total portfolio value
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_holdings_value, (SELECT SUM(h2.quantity * h2.current_price) FROM holdings h2 JOIN portfolios p2 ON h2.portfolio_id = p2.portfolio_id WHERE p2.investor_id = i.investor_id) AS total_portfolio_value, NTILE(4) OVER(ORDER BY (SELECT SUM(h2.quantity * h2.current_price) FROM holdings h2 JOIN portfolios p2 ON h2.portfolio_id = p2.portfolio_id WHERE p2.investor_id = i.investor_id) / SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price)) AS quartile FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id) SELECT investor_id, total_holdings_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE quartile = 1

question: What are the top 10 instruments by total trade quantity, considering only trades made in the last month and excluding instruments with a market capitalization less than the average market capitalization of all instruments?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.symbol, SUM(t.quantity) AS total_trade_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(t.quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > (SELECT CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND i.market_cap_cr > (SELECT AVG(market_cap_cr) FROM instruments) GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.symbol) SELECT instrument_id, symbol, total_trade_quantity FROM ranked_instruments WHERE row_num <= 10

question: List all portfolios with their total trade quantity, and rank them by their total trade quantity as a percentage of their total holdings quantity
sql: WITH ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, SUM(t.quantity) AS total_trade_quantity, (SELECT SUM(h.quantity) FROM holdings h WHERE h.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id) AS total_holdings_quantity, NTILE(4) OVER(ORDER BY (SELECT SUM(t2.quantity) FROM trades t2 WHERE t2.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id) / (SELECT SUM(h2.quantity) FROM holdings h2 WHERE h2.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id)) AS quartile FROM portfolios p JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id) SELECT portfolio_id, total_trade_quantity FROM ranked_portfolios WHERE quartile = 1

question: What are the top 5 investors by total portfolio value, considering only portfolios with more than one instrument and excluding investors with a risk profile of 'low'?
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_portfolio_value, DENSE_RANK() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id WHERE i.risk_profile != 'low' AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT h2.instrument_id) FROM holdings h2 WHERE h2.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id) > 1 GROUP BY i.investor_id) SELECT investor_id, total_portfolio_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE rank <= 5

question: What are the top 3 portfolios for each investor based on the total market value of their holdings, along with the average buy price and current price of each holding?
sql: WITH ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY p.investor_id ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id) SELECT rp.investor_id, rp.portfolio_id, h.avg_buy_price, h.current_price FROM ranked_portfolios rp JOIN holdings h ON rp.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id WHERE rp.row_num <= 3

question: What is the total market value of each portfolio, and what is the rank of each portfolio within its investor group based on this value?
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id) SELECT pv.investor_id, pv.portfolio_id, pv.total_market_value, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY pv.investor_id ORDER BY pv.total_market_value DESC) AS rank FROM portfolio_values pv

question: Which investors have the highest total market value across all their portfolios, and what is the average market cap of the instruments in their portfolios?
sql: WITH investor_values AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT iv.investor_id, iv.total_market_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY iv.total_market_value DESC) AS row_num FROM investor_values iv) SELECT ri.investor_id, ri.total_market_value, AVG(i.market_cap_cr) AS avg_market_cap FROM ranked_investors ri JOIN portfolios p ON ri.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE ri.row_num <= 10 GROUP BY ri.investor_id, ri.total_market_value

question: Which portfolios have the highest total market value, and what is the rank of each portfolio within its investor group based on this value?
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id), ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT pv.investor_id, pv.portfolio_id, pv.total_market_value, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY pv.investor_id ORDER BY pv.total_market_value DESC) AS rank FROM portfolio_values pv) SELECT rp.investor_id, rp.portfolio_id, rp.total_market_value, rp.rank FROM ranked_portfolios rp WHERE rp.total_market_value > (SELECT AVG(total_market_value) FROM portfolio_values) ORDER BY rp.total_market_value DESC

question: What is the total market value of each instrument, and what is the percentage of each instrument's market value relative to the total market value of all instruments?
sql: WITH instrument_values AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM instruments i JOIN holdings h ON i.instrument_id = h.instrument_id GROUP BY i.instrument_id), total_value AS (SELECT SUM(total_market_value) AS total FROM instrument_values) SELECT iv.instrument_id, iv.total_market_value, (iv.total_market_value / tv.total) * 100 AS percentage FROM instrument_values iv CROSS JOIN total_value tv

question: Which investors have the highest average market value per portfolio, and what is the total market value of each portfolio?
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id), investor_averages AS (SELECT pv.investor_id, AVG(pv.total_market_value) AS avg_market_value FROM portfolio_values pv GROUP BY pv.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT ia.investor_id, ia.avg_market_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY ia.avg_market_value DESC) AS row_num FROM investor_averages ia) SELECT ri.investor_id, pv.portfolio_id, pv.total_market_value FROM ranked_investors ri JOIN portfolio_values pv ON ri.investor_id = pv.investor_id WHERE ri.row_num <= 10

question: Which portfolios have the highest total market value, and what is the average market cap of the instruments in each portfolio?
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_market_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.investor_id, p.portfolio_id), ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT pv.investor_id, pv.portfolio_id, pv.total_market_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY pv.total_market_value DESC) AS row_num FROM portfolio_values pv) SELECT rp.investor_id, rp.portfolio_id, rp.total_market_value, AVG(i.market_cap_cr) AS avg_market_cap FROM ranked_portfolios rp JOIN holdings h ON rp.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE rp.row_num <= 10 GROUP BY rp.investor_id, rp.portfolio_id, rp.total_market_value

question: What is the top 10% of instruments by average holding value per investor, considering only investors with a high risk profile?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) AS avg_holding_value, NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(h.quantity * h.avg_buy_price) DESC) AS ntile FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id JOIN instruments i ON h.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE inv.risk_profile = 'High' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT instrument_id, name, avg_holding_value FROM ranked_instruments WHERE ntile = 1

question: List all investors with their total portfolio value, ranked by the total portfolio value, and filter out investors with less than 5 trades in the last month
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT inv.investor_id, inv.full_name, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_portfolio_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id WHERE inv.investor_id IN (SELECT inv.investor_id FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 month' GROUP BY inv.investor_id HAVING COUNT(t.trade_id) >= 5) GROUP BY inv.investor_id, inv.full_name) SELECT investor_id, full_name, total_portfolio_value FROM ranked_investors

question: What are the top 3 instruments by average daily trade value for each sector, considering only instruments with a market cap greater than 1000 cr?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.sector, i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(t.price * t.quantity) AS avg_daily_trade_value, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY i.sector ORDER BY AVG(t.price * t.quantity) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 1000 GROUP BY i.sector, i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT sector, instrument_id, name, avg_daily_trade_value FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank <= 3

question: Find the investor with the highest total portfolio value, considering only investors with a low risk profile and at least 2 portfolios
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT inv.investor_id, inv.full_name, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_portfolio_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id WHERE inv.risk_profile = 'Low' AND inv.investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM portfolios GROUP BY investor_id HAVING COUNT(portfolio_id) >= 2) GROUP BY inv.investor_id, inv.full_name) SELECT investor_id, full_name, total_portfolio_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE row_num = 1

question: List all instruments with their average holding quantity, ranked by the average holding quantity, and filter out instruments with less than 10 holdings
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(h.quantity) AS avg_holding_quantity, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(h.quantity) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN holdings h ON i.instrument_id = h.instrument_id GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name HAVING COUNT(h.holding_id) >= 10) SELECT instrument_id, name, avg_holding_quantity FROM ranked_instruments

question: What is the total trade value for each instrument, considering only trades made in the last quarter, and rank the instruments by the total trade value?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, SUM(t.price * t.quantity) AS total_trade_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(t.price * t.quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '3 month' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT instrument_id, name, total_trade_value FROM ranked_instruments

question: Find the top 5 sectors by average instrument market cap, considering only instruments with a market cap greater than 500 cr
sql: WITH ranked_sectors AS (SELECT i.sector, AVG(i.market_cap_cr) AS avg_market_cap, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(i.market_cap_cr) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 500 GROUP BY i.sector) SELECT sector, avg_market_cap FROM ranked_sectors WHERE rank <= 5

question: List all investors with their total portfolio value, ranked by the total portfolio value, and filter out investors with less than 10000 cr in total portfolio value
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT inv.investor_id, inv.full_name, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_portfolio_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS row_num FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY inv.investor_id, inv.full_name HAVING SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) >= 10000) SELECT investor_id, full_name, total_portfolio_value FROM ranked_investors

question: What are the top 2 instruments by average trade quantity for each sector, considering only instruments with a market cap greater than 1000 cr?
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.sector, i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(t.quantity) AS avg_trade_quantity, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY i.sector ORDER BY AVG(t.quantity) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN trades t ON i.instrument_id = t.instrument_id WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 1000 GROUP BY i.sector, i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT sector, instrument_id, name, avg_trade_quantity FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank <= 2

question: Find the investor with the highest total trade value, considering only investors with a medium risk profile and at least 5 trades in the last month
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT inv.investor_id, inv.full_name, SUM(t.price * t.quantity) AS total_trade_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(t.price * t.quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id WHERE inv.risk_profile = 'Medium' AND t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 month' AND inv.investor_id IN (SELECT inv.investor_id FROM investors inv JOIN portfolios p ON inv.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN trades t ON p.portfolio_id = t.portfolio_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 month' GROUP BY inv.investor_id HAVING COUNT(t.trade_id) >= 5) GROUP BY inv.investor_id, inv.full_name) SELECT investor_id, full_name, total_trade_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE row_num = 1

question: List all investors with their total portfolio value, ranked by the total portfolio value, and only include investors with more than one portfolio
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, total_value, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS rank FROM portfolio_values WHERE investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM portfolios GROUP BY investor_id HAVING COUNT(portfolio_id) > 1)) SELECT investor_id, total_value FROM ranked_investors

question: Find the average trade quantity for each instrument type, considering only trades made in the last 30 days, and rank the instrument types by average trade quantity
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT t.instrument_id, t.quantity, i.instrument_type FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'), ranked_instrument_types AS (SELECT instrument_type, AVG(quantity) AS avg_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(quantity) DESC) AS row_num FROM recent_trades GROUP BY instrument_type) SELECT instrument_type, avg_quantity FROM ranked_instrument_types

question: What is the total market cap of all instruments in each sector, considering only sectors with more than 3 instruments, and rank the sectors by total market cap?
sql: WITH sector_market_cap AS (SELECT i.sector, SUM(i.market_cap_cr) AS total_market_cap FROM instruments i WHERE i.sector IN (SELECT sector FROM instruments GROUP BY sector HAVING COUNT(instrument_id) > 3) GROUP BY i.sector), ranked_sectors AS (SELECT sector, total_market_cap, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_market_cap DESC) AS rank FROM sector_market_cap) SELECT sector, total_market_cap FROM ranked_sectors

question: List all portfolios with their total value, ranked by the total value, and only include portfolios with more than 5 holdings
sql: WITH portfolio_values AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id), ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT portfolio_id, total_value, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS quartile FROM portfolio_values WHERE portfolio_id IN (SELECT portfolio_id FROM holdings GROUP BY portfolio_id HAVING COUNT(holding_id) > 5)) SELECT portfolio_id, total_value FROM ranked_portfolios

question: Find the top 2 investors by total portfolio value in each risk profile, considering only investors with more than one portfolio
sql: WITH investor_values AS (SELECT i.investor_id, i.risk_profile, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id, i.risk_profile), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, risk_profile, total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY risk_profile ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS row_num FROM investor_values WHERE investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM portfolios GROUP BY investor_id HAVING COUNT(portfolio_id) > 1)) SELECT investor_id, risk_profile, total_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE row_num <= 2

question: What is the average brokerage paid for each instrument type, considering only trades made in the last 60 days, and rank the instrument types by average brokerage?
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT t.instrument_id, t.brokerage, i.instrument_type FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'), ranked_instrument_types AS (SELECT instrument_type, AVG(brokerage) AS avg_brokerage, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(brokerage) DESC) AS rank FROM recent_trades GROUP BY instrument_type) SELECT instrument_type, avg_brokerage FROM ranked_instrument_types

question: List all instruments with their average trade quantity, ranked by the average trade quantity, and only include instruments with more than 10 trades
sql: WITH instrument_trades AS (SELECT t.instrument_id, AVG(t.quantity) AS avg_quantity FROM trades t GROUP BY t.instrument_id), ranked_instruments AS (SELECT instrument_id, avg_quantity, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY avg_quantity DESC) AS quartile FROM instrument_trades WHERE instrument_id IN (SELECT instrument_id FROM trades GROUP BY instrument_id HAVING COUNT(trade_id) > 10)) SELECT instrument_id, avg_quantity FROM ranked_instruments

question: Find the total value of all portfolios for each investor, considering only investors with more than one portfolio, and rank the investors by total portfolio value
sql: WITH investor_values AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, total_value, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_value DESC) AS rank FROM investor_values WHERE investor_id IN (SELECT investor_id FROM portfolios GROUP BY investor_id HAVING COUNT(portfolio_id) > 1)) SELECT investor_id, total_value FROM ranked_investors

question: What is the average market cap of all instruments in each sector, considering only sectors with more than 2 instruments, and rank the sectors by average market cap?
sql: WITH sector_market_cap AS (SELECT i.sector, AVG(i.market_cap_cr) AS avg_market_cap FROM instruments i WHERE i.sector IN (SELECT sector FROM instruments GROUP BY sector HAVING COUNT(instrument_id) > 2) GROUP BY i.sector), ranked_sectors AS (SELECT sector, avg_market_cap, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_market_cap DESC) AS rank FROM sector_market_cap) SELECT sector, avg_market_cap FROM ranked_sectors

question: Which investors have the highest total portfolio value, with a ranking of their portfolio values?
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT i.full_name, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.full_name) SELECT full_name, total_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE rank <= 10

question: What is the average daily brokerage for each instrument type, considering only trades with a status of 'executed' and a quantity greater than 100?
sql: WITH daily_brokerage AS (SELECT instrument_id, DATE(traded_at) AS trade_date, SUM(brokerage) AS total_brokerage FROM trades WHERE status = 'executed' AND quantity > 100 GROUP BY instrument_id, DATE(traded_at)), ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_type, AVG(db.total_brokerage) AS avg_brokerage, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY i.instrument_type ORDER BY AVG(db.total_brokerage) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN daily_brokerage db ON i.instrument_id = db.instrument_id GROUP BY i.instrument_type) SELECT instrument_type, avg_brokerage FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank = 1

question: List the top 5 investors by total portfolio value, with their corresponding risk profile and the number of portfolios they own.
sql: WITH ranked_investors AS (SELECT i.investor_id, i.full_name, i.risk_profile, COUNT(p.portfolio_id) AS num_portfolios, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM investors i JOIN portfolios p ON i.investor_id = p.investor_id JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY i.investor_id, i.full_name, i.risk_profile) SELECT full_name, risk_profile, num_portfolios, total_value FROM ranked_investors WHERE rank <= 5

question: Find the instrument with the highest average current price among all instruments, considering only those with a market cap greater than 1000 cr.
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(h.current_price) AS avg_price, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN holdings h ON i.instrument_id = h.instrument_id WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 1000 GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT name, avg_price FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank = 1

question: What is the total quantity of each instrument type traded in the last 30 days, with a ranking of the instrument types by total quantity?
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT i.instrument_type, SUM(t.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM trades t JOIN instruments i ON t.instrument_id = i.instrument_id WHERE t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY i.instrument_type), ranked_instruments AS (SELECT instrument_type, total_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_quantity DESC) AS rank FROM recent_trades) SELECT instrument_type, total_quantity FROM ranked_instruments

question: List the investors with the highest total brokerage paid in the last 60 days, considering only trades with a status of 'executed'.
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(t.brokerage) AS total_brokerage FROM trades t JOIN portfolios p ON t.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id JOIN investors i ON p.investor_id = i.investor_id WHERE t.status = 'executed' AND t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, total_brokerage, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_brokerage DESC) AS rank FROM recent_trades) SELECT i.full_name, total_brokerage FROM ranked_investors ri JOIN investors i ON ri.investor_id = i.investor_id WHERE rank <= 10

question: Find the top 3 instruments by average current price among all instruments, considering only those with a market cap greater than 500 cr and a sector of 'Technology'.
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(h.current_price) AS avg_price, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN holdings h ON i.instrument_id = h.instrument_id WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 500 AND i.sector = 'Technology' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT name, avg_price FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank <= 3

question: What is the total value of each portfolio, with a ranking of the portfolios by total value?
sql: WITH ranked_portfolios AS (SELECT p.portfolio_id, SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) AS total_value, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(h.quantity * h.current_price) DESC) AS rank FROM portfolios p JOIN holdings h ON p.portfolio_id = h.portfolio_id GROUP BY p.portfolio_id) SELECT total_value FROM ranked_portfolios

question: List the investors with the highest total quantity of instruments traded in the last 90 days, considering only trades with a status of 'executed'.
sql: WITH recent_trades AS (SELECT i.investor_id, SUM(t.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM trades t JOIN portfolios p ON t.portfolio_id = p.portfolio_id JOIN investors i ON p.investor_id = i.investor_id WHERE t.status = 'executed' AND t.traded_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY i.investor_id), ranked_investors AS (SELECT investor_id, total_quantity, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_quantity DESC) AS rank FROM recent_trades) SELECT i.full_name, total_quantity FROM ranked_investors ri JOIN investors i ON ri.investor_id = i.investor_id WHERE rank <= 10

question: Find the instrument with the lowest average current price among all instruments, considering only those with a market cap greater than 2000 cr and a sector of 'Finance'.
sql: WITH ranked_instruments AS (SELECT i.instrument_id, i.name, AVG(h.current_price) AS avg_price, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(h.current_price) ASC) AS rank FROM instruments i JOIN holdings h ON i.instrument_id = h.instrument_id WHERE i.market_cap_cr > 2000 AND i.sector = 'Finance' GROUP BY i.instrument_id, i.name) SELECT name, avg_price FROM ranked_instruments WHERE rank = 1

question: What are the top 3 cities with the highest average sum insured per policy, ranked by average sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT city, AVG(sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, avg_sum_insured FROM ranked_cities ORDER BY avg_sum_insured DESC LIMIT 3

question: List all policyholders who have purchased more than one policy, with the total sum insured for each policyholder, ranked by total sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) > 1) SELECT holder_id, full_name, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_policyholders ORDER BY rank

question: Find the average claim amount for each claim type, with a ranking of claim types by average claim amount?
sql: WITH ranked_claim_types AS (SELECT claim_type, AVG(claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c GROUP BY claim_type) SELECT claim_type, avg_claim_amount FROM ranked_claim_types ORDER BY rank

question: What is the total sum insured for each product type, with a ranking of product types by total sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_product_types AS (SELECT ip.type, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS quartile FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.type) SELECT type, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_product_types ORDER BY total_sum_insured DESC

question: List all policyholders who have filed a claim, with the total claim amount for each policyholder, ranked by total claim amount?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name) SELECT holder_id, full_name, total_claim_amount FROM ranked_policyholders ORDER BY rank

question: Find the average premium for each city, with a ranking of cities by average premium?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT ph.city, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, avg_premium FROM ranked_cities ORDER BY rank

question: Show me the top 10% of policyholders with the highest total sum insured, ranked by total sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS percentile FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name) SELECT holder_id, full_name, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE percentile = 1 ORDER BY total_sum_insured DESC

question: What is the total claim amount for each agent, with a ranking of agents by total claim amount?
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN agents a ON p.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM insurance_products WHERE type = 'life') GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name) SELECT agent_id, name, total_claim_amount FROM ranked_agents ORDER BY rank

question: List all insurance products with the average sum insured for each product, ranked by average sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name) SELECT product_id, name, avg_sum_insured FROM ranked_products ORDER BY rank

question: Which policyholders have the highest average claim amount per policy, and what is their rank?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.holder_id, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.holder_id ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM policies p JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY p.holder_id) SELECT ph.full_name, rp.avg_claim, rp.row_num FROM ranked_policies rp JOIN policyholders ph ON rp.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE rp.row_num IN (SELECT row_num FROM ranked_policies WHERE row_num <= 5)

question: List the top 3 insurance products by total sum insured, along with their product names and average premium
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name) SELECT rp.name, rp.total_sum, rp.avg_premium FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.rank <= 3

question: Show me the policyholders with more than one policy, along with their policy count and average sum insured
sql: WITH policy_counts AS (SELECT p.holder_id, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum FROM policies p GROUP BY p.holder_id HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) > 1) SELECT ph.full_name, pc.policy_count, pc.avg_sum FROM policy_counts pc JOIN policyholders ph ON pc.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE pc.policy_count > (SELECT AVG(policy_count) FROM policy_counts)

question: Find the agents with the highest commission percentage, and list their names, regions, and total active policies
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, a.commission_pct, a.active_policies, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY a.commission_pct DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a) SELECT ra.name, ra.region, ra.active_policies FROM ranked_agents ra WHERE ra.row_num IN (SELECT row_num FROM ranked_agents WHERE row_num <= 5) AND ra.agent_id IN (SELECT agent_id FROM (SELECT a.agent_id, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id) AS subquery WHERE policy_count > 10)

question: What are the top 5 cities with the highest average sum insured per policy, and what is their rank?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT ph.city, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT rc.city, rc.avg_sum, rc.rank FROM ranked_cities rc WHERE rc.rank <= 5

question: List all policyholders with a claim amount greater than the average claim amount for their product type
sql: WITH avg_claims AS (SELECT ip.type, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ip.type) SELECT ph.full_name, c.claim_amount FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE c.claim_amount > (SELECT avg_claim FROM avg_claims WHERE type = ip.type) AND ip.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM (SELECT ip.product_id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ip.type ORDER BY ip.min_premium) AS row_num FROM insurance_products ip) AS subquery WHERE row_num = 1)

question: Which insurance products have the highest total sum insured, and what is their average premium?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS ntile FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name) SELECT rp.name, rp.total_sum, rp.avg_premium FROM ranked_products rp WHERE rp.ntile = 1 AND rp.product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM (SELECT ip.product_id, MAX(ip.max_sum_insured) AS max_sum FROM insurance_products ip GROUP BY ip.product_id) AS subquery)

question: Find the policyholders with the highest total claim amount, and list their names, total claim amount, and rank
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.holder_id, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY p.holder_id) SELECT ph.full_name, rp.total_claim, rp.rank FROM ranked_policies rp JOIN policyholders ph ON rp.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE rp.rank <= 5 AND rp.holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM (SELECT p.holder_id, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count FROM policies p GROUP BY p.holder_id) AS subquery WHERE policy_count > 2)

question: List all agents with an active policy count greater than the average active policy count, along with their names, regions, and active policy count
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, a.active_policies, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY a.active_policies DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a) SELECT ra.name, ra.region, ra.active_policies FROM ranked_agents ra WHERE ra.active_policies > (SELECT AVG(active_policies) FROM agents) AND ra.agent_id IN (SELECT agent_id FROM (SELECT a.agent_id, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id) AS subquery WHERE total_sum > 10000)

question: Show me the policyholders with the highest average claim amount per policy, along with their names, average claim amount, and rank
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.holder_id, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY p.holder_id) SELECT ph.full_name, rp.avg_claim, rp.rank FROM ranked_policies rp JOIN policyholders ph ON rp.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE rp.rank <= 5 AND rp.holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM (SELECT p.holder_id, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count FROM policies p GROUP BY p.holder_id) AS subquery WHERE policy_count > 1)

question: What are the top 3 policyholders with the highest sum insured in each city, along with their total premium paid and rank?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS ( SELECT ph.city, ph.full_name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city, ph.full_name ) SELECT city, full_name, total_sum_insured, total_premium, rank FROM ranked_policies WHERE rank <= 3 AND city IN ( SELECT city FROM policyholders WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE premium_annual > ( SELECT AVG(premium_annual) FROM policies ) ) )

question: List the top 3 insurance products by total sum insured, along with the number of policies and average premium, where the product has at least 5 policies?
sql: WITH product_ranks AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, SUM(pol.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, COUNT(pol.policy_id) AS num_policies, AVG(pol.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(pol.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies pol ON ip.product_id = pol.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name HAVING COUNT(pol.policy_id) >= 5) SELECT product_id, name, total_sum_insured, num_policies, avg_premium FROM product_ranks WHERE rank <= 3

question: What are the top 2 cities with the highest average claim amount, along with the total claim amount and number of claims, where the claim amount is greater than the average claim amount for the city?
sql: WITH city_ranks AS (SELECT p.city, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, COUNT(c.claim_id) AS num_claims, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies pol ON c.policy_id = pol.policy_id JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE c.claim_amount > (SELECT AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims) GROUP BY p.city HAVING COUNT(c.claim_id) >= 5) SELECT city, avg_claim_amount, total_claim_amount, num_claims FROM city_ranks WHERE rank <= 2

question: Show me the agents with the highest total commission earned in each region, along with their total commission and rank?
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, SUM(pol.premium_annual * a.commission_pct) AS total_commission, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a.region ORDER BY SUM(pol.premium_annual * a.commission_pct) DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a JOIN policies pol ON a.agent_id = pol.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name, a.region) SELECT agent_id, name, region, total_commission FROM ranked_agents WHERE row_num = 1

question: Find the policies with the highest sum insured in each city, along with the policyholder's name and the sum insured, where the sum insured is greater than the average sum insured for the city?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.holder_id, p.full_name, pol.policy_id, pol.sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY pol.sum_insured DESC) AS row_num FROM policies pol JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE pol.sum_insured > (SELECT AVG(sum_insured) FROM policies)) SELECT holder_id, full_name, policy_id, sum_insured FROM ranked_policies WHERE row_num = 1

question: List the top 3 policyholders with the highest total claim amount in each city, along with their total claim amount and rank?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT p.holder_id, p.full_name, p.city, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders p JOIN policies pol ON p.holder_id = pol.holder_id JOIN claims c ON pol.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY p.holder_id, p.full_name, p.city) SELECT holder_id, full_name, city, total_claim_amount FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank <= 3

question: Find the agents with the highest total active policies in each region, along with their total active policies and rank?
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.agent_id, a.name, a.region, SUM(pol.status = 'active') AS total_active_policies, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a.region ORDER BY SUM(pol.status = 'active') DESC) AS row_num FROM agents a JOIN policies pol ON a.agent_id = pol.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.name, a.region) SELECT agent_id, name, region, total_active_policies FROM ranked_agents WHERE row_num = 1

question: Show me the insurance products with the lowest average premium in each city, along with the average premium and number of policies, where the premium is less than the average premium for the city?
sql: WITH product_ranks AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.name, p.city, AVG(pol.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, COUNT(pol.policy_id) AS num_policies, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY AVG(pol.premium_annual) ASC) AS row_num FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies pol ON ip.product_id = pol.product_id JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE pol.premium_annual < (SELECT AVG(premium_annual) FROM policies) GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.name, p.city) SELECT product_id, name, city, avg_premium, num_policies FROM product_ranks WHERE row_num = 1

question: What are the top 3 cities by policy count, with the total premium and average sum insured, and the row number of each city?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT city, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(p.policy_id) DESC) AS row_num FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, policy_count, total_premium, avg_sum_insured, row_num FROM ranked_cities WHERE row_num <= 3 AND city IN (SELECT city FROM policyholders WHERE holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: Find the average claim amount for each region, with the rank of each region by average claim amount, and the total claim amount for each region?
sql: WITH ranked_regions AS (SELECT a.region, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = (SELECT agent_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id = p.policy_id) JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY a.region) SELECT region, avg_claim_amount, total_claim_amount, rank FROM ranked_regions WHERE region IN (SELECT region FROM agents WHERE active_policies > (SELECT AVG(active_policies) FROM agents))

question: List the top 5 insurance products by total sum insured, with the total premium and average tenure, and the dense rank of each product?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, AVG(ip.tenure_years) AS avg_tenure, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS dense_rank FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.name) SELECT name, total_sum_insured, total_premium, avg_tenure, dense_rank FROM ranked_products WHERE dense_rank <= 5 AND name IN (SELECT name FROM insurance_products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: Find the total claim amount for each policy status, with the average claim amount and the row number of each status?
sql: WITH ranked_statuses AS (SELECT p.status, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM policies p JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY p.status) SELECT status, total_claim_amount, avg_claim_amount, row_num FROM ranked_statuses WHERE row_num <= 3 AND status IN (SELECT status FROM policies WHERE policy_id IN (SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE claim_type = 'accident'))

question: What are the top 2 occupations by policy count, with the total premium and average sum insured, and the ntile of each occupation?
sql: WITH ranked_occupations AS (SELECT ph.occupation, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(p.policy_id) DESC) AS ntile FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.occupation) SELECT occupation, policy_count, total_premium, avg_sum_insured, ntile FROM ranked_occupations WHERE ntile <= 2 AND occupation IN (SELECT occupation FROM policyholders WHERE holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: Find the average premium for each region, with the rank of each region by average premium, and the total premium for each region?
sql: WITH ranked_regions AS (SELECT a.region, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS rank FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = (SELECT agent_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id = p.policy_id) GROUP BY a.region) SELECT region, avg_premium, total_premium, rank FROM ranked_regions WHERE region IN (SELECT region FROM agents WHERE active_policies > (SELECT AVG(active_policies) FROM agents))

question: List the bottom 3 insurance products by total sum insured, with the total premium and average tenure, and the row number of each product?
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.name, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, AVG(ip.tenure_years) AS avg_tenure, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) ASC) AS row_num FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.name) SELECT name, total_sum_insured, total_premium, avg_tenure, row_num FROM ranked_products WHERE row_num <= 3 AND name IN (SELECT name FROM insurance_products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: Find the total policy count for each city, with the average sum insured and the dense rank of each city?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT ph.city, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count, AVG(p.sum_insured) AS avg_sum_insured, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(p.policy_id) DESC) AS dense_rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, policy_count, avg_sum_insured, dense_rank FROM ranked_cities WHERE dense_rank <= 5 AND city IN (SELECT city FROM policyholders WHERE holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: What are the top 5 agents by total premium, with the total policy count and average commission, and the ntile of each agent?
sql: WITH ranked_agents AS (SELECT a.name, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS policy_count, AVG(a.commission_pct) AS avg_commission, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS ntile FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = (SELECT agent_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id = p.policy_id) GROUP BY a.name) SELECT name, total_premium, policy_count, avg_commission, ntile FROM ranked_agents WHERE ntile <= 2 AND name IN (SELECT name FROM agents WHERE active_policies > (SELECT AVG(active_policies) FROM agents))

question: Find the average claim amount for each policy type, with the rank of each policy type by average claim amount, and the total claim amount for each policy type?
sql: WITH ranked_policy_types AS (SELECT ip.type, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ip.type) SELECT type, avg_claim_amount, total_claim_amount, rank FROM ranked_policy_types WHERE type IN (SELECT type FROM insurance_products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE status = 'active'))

question: Which policyholders have the highest total sum insured in their city, and what is their rank in terms of total sum insured?
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS ( SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.city, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.city ) SELECT holder_id, city, total_sum_insured, rank FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank = 1 AND holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id IN ( SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE claim_amount > ( SELECT AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims ) ) )

question: List all insurance products with their average premium, and show the top 3 products with the highest average premium in each product type
sql: WITH avg_premiums AS ( SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type ), ranked_products AS ( SELECT product_id, type, avg_premium, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY avg_premium DESC) AS rank FROM avg_premiums ) SELECT product_id, type, avg_premium FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 3 AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE city IN ( SELECT city FROM policyholders GROUP BY city HAVING COUNT(holder_id) > 100 ) ) )

question: Find the total sum insured for each policyholder, and show the policyholders with the highest total sum insured in each occupation
sql: WITH total_sum_insured AS ( SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.occupation, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.occupation ), ranked_policyholders AS ( SELECT holder_id, occupation, total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation ORDER BY total_sum_insured DESC) AS rank FROM total_sum_insured ) SELECT holder_id, occupation, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank = 1 AND holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id IN ( SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE claim_type IN ( SELECT claim_type FROM claims GROUP BY claim_type HAVING COUNT(claim_id) > 50 ) ) )

question: What is the average claim amount for each insurance product, and show the top 2 products with the highest average claim amount in each product type
sql: WITH avg_claim_amounts AS ( SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type ), ranked_products AS ( SELECT product_id, type, avg_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY avg_claim_amount DESC) AS rank FROM avg_claim_amounts ) SELECT product_id, type, avg_claim_amount FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 2 AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE dob BETWEEN '1980-01-01' AND '1990-12-31' ) )

question: List all agents with their total commission, and show the top agent with the highest total commission in each region
sql: WITH total_commission AS ( SELECT a.agent_id, a.region, SUM(p.premium_annual * a.commission_pct) AS total_commission FROM agents a JOIN policies p ON a.agent_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY a.agent_id, a.region ), ranked_agents AS ( SELECT agent_id, region, total_commission, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_commission DESC) AS rank FROM total_commission ) SELECT agent_id, region, total_commission FROM ranked_agents WHERE rank = 1 AND agent_id IN ( SELECT agent_id FROM agents WHERE joined_date > '2010-01-01' AND license_no IN ( SELECT license_no FROM agents GROUP BY license_no HAVING COUNT(agent_id) = 1 ) )

question: Find the total number of policies for each insurance product, and show the products with the highest total number of policies in each product type
sql: WITH total_policies AS ( SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS total_policies FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type ), ranked_products AS ( SELECT product_id, type, total_policies, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY total_policies DESC) AS rank FROM total_policies ) SELECT product_id, type, total_policies FROM ranked_products WHERE rank = 1 AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE occupation IN ( SELECT occupation FROM policyholders GROUP BY occupation HAVING COUNT(holder_id) > 500 ) ) )

question: What is the average premium for each policyholder, and show the policyholders with the highest average premium in each city
sql: WITH avg_premiums AS ( SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.city, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.city ), ranked_policyholders AS ( SELECT holder_id, city, avg_premium, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY city ORDER BY avg_premium DESC) AS rank FROM avg_premiums ) SELECT holder_id, city, avg_premium FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank = 1 AND holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id IN ( SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE settled_amount > ( SELECT AVG(settled_amount) FROM claims ) ) )

question: List all insurance products with their total sum insured, and show the top 2 products with the highest total sum insured in each product type
sql: WITH total_sum_insured AS ( SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type ), ranked_products AS ( SELECT product_id, type, total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY total_sum_insured DESC) AS rank FROM total_sum_insured ) SELECT product_id, type, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_products WHERE rank <= 2 AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE email LIKE '%@gmail.com' ) )

question: Find the total claim amount for each policyholder, and show the policyholders with the highest total claim amount in each occupation
sql: WITH total_claim_amount AS ( SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.occupation, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.occupation ), ranked_policyholders AS ( SELECT holder_id, occupation, total_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY occupation ORDER BY total_claim_amount DESC) AS rank FROM total_claim_amount ) SELECT holder_id, occupation, total_claim_amount FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank = 1 AND holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE policy_id IN ( SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE claim_type IN ( SELECT claim_type FROM claims GROUP BY claim_type HAVING COUNT(claim_id) > 20 ) ) )

question: What is the average settlement amount for each insurance product, and show the top product with the highest average settlement amount in each product type
sql: WITH avg_settlement_amounts AS ( SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, AVG(c.settled_amount) AS avg_settlement_amount FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id JOIN claims c ON p.policy_id = c.policy_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type ), ranked_products AS ( SELECT product_id, type, avg_settlement_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY type ORDER BY avg_settlement_amount DESC) AS rank FROM avg_settlement_amounts ) SELECT product_id, type, avg_settlement_amount FROM ranked_products WHERE rank = 1 AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE holder_id IN ( SELECT holder_id FROM policyholders WHERE city IN ( SELECT city FROM policyholders GROUP BY city HAVING COUNT(holder_id) > 200 ) ) )

question: List all policyholders who have purchased more than one policy, along with the total premium they have paid and their ranking within their city based on the total premium paid
sql: WITH ranked_holders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city HAVING COUNT(p.policy_id) > 1) SELECT holder_id, full_name, city, total_premium, rank FROM ranked_holders

question: Find the average claim amount for each claim type, considering only claims that have been settled, and rank the claim types by their average claim amount in descending order
sql: WITH ranked_claims AS (SELECT c.claim_type, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, RANK() OVER(ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c WHERE c.status = 'settled' GROUP BY c.claim_type) SELECT claim_type, avg_claim_amount, rank FROM ranked_claims

question: Show me the total sum insured for each insurance product, along with the ranking of each product within its type based on the total sum insured, and considering only policies with a status of 'active'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.name, ip.type, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ip.type ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ip.name, ip.type) SELECT name, type, total_sum_insured, rank FROM ranked_products

question: List all policyholders who have filed a claim, along with the total claim amount they have filed and their ranking within their city based on the total claim amount filed
sql: WITH ranked_holders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city) SELECT holder_id, full_name, city, total_claim_amount, rank FROM ranked_holders

question: Find the total premium paid for each insurance product, along with the ranking of each product within its type based on the total premium paid, and considering only policies with a status of 'active'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.name, ip.type, SUM(p.premium_annual) AS total_premium, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ip.type ORDER BY SUM(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ip.name, ip.type) SELECT name, type, total_premium, rank FROM ranked_products

question: Show me the total sum insured for each policyholder, along with the ranking of each policyholder within their city based on the total sum insured, and considering only policies with a status of 'active'
sql: WITH ranked_holders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policies p JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.full_name, ph.city) SELECT holder_id, full_name, city, total_sum_insured, rank FROM ranked_holders

question: What are the top 3 cities by total claim amount filed, considering only claims that have been settled?
sql: WITH ranked_cities AS (SELECT ph.city, SUM(c.claim_amount) AS total_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS row_num FROM claims c JOIN policies p ON c.policy_id = p.policy_id JOIN policyholders ph ON p.holder_id = ph.holder_id WHERE c.status = 'settled' GROUP BY ph.city) SELECT city, total_claim_amount FROM ranked_cities WHERE row_num <= 3

question: List all insurance products that have a total sum insured greater than the average total sum insured for their type, considering only policies with a status of 'active'
sql: WITH ranked_products AS (SELECT ip.name, ip.type, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id WHERE p.status = 'active' GROUP BY ip.name, ip.type) SELECT name, type, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_products WHERE total_sum_insured > (SELECT AVG(total_sum_insured) FROM ranked_products rp2 WHERE rp2.type = ranked_products.type)

question: Find the claim type with the highest average claim amount per policyholder in each city
sql: WITH ranked_claim_types AS (SELECT c.claim_type, p.city, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies pol ON c.policy_id = pol.policy_id JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY c.claim_type, p.city) SELECT claim_type, city, avg_claim_amount FROM ranked_claim_types WHERE rank = 1 AND claim_type IN (SELECT claim_type FROM claims WHERE claim_amount > (SELECT AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims))

question: What is the average claim amount per policy for policies with a claim type in the top 3 claim types by average claim amount in each city?
sql: WITH ranked_claim_types AS (SELECT c.claim_type, p.city, AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY AVG(c.claim_amount) DESC) AS rank FROM claims c JOIN policies pol ON c.policy_id = pol.policy_id JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY c.claim_type, p.city) SELECT AVG(c.claim_amount) AS avg_claim_amount FROM claims c JOIN policies pol ON c.policy_id = pol.policy_id JOIN policyholders p ON pol.holder_id = p.holder_id WHERE c.claim_type IN (SELECT claim_type FROM ranked_claim_types WHERE rank <= 3) AND c.claim_type IN (SELECT claim_type FROM claims WHERE claim_amount > (SELECT AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims))

question: Find the policyholder with the highest total sum insured in each city
sql: WITH ranked_policyholders AS (SELECT p.holder_id, p.full_name, p.city, SUM(pol.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.city ORDER BY SUM(pol.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders p JOIN policies pol ON p.holder_id = pol.holder_id GROUP BY p.holder_id, p.full_name, p.city) SELECT full_name, city, total_sum_insured FROM ranked_policyholders WHERE rank = 1 AND holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE sum_insured > (SELECT AVG(sum_insured) FROM policies))

question: What is the total premium annual for policies with a premium annual above the average premium annual in each insurance product?
sql: WITH ranked_policies AS (SELECT p.policy_id, p.premium_annual, ip.product_id, ip.name, AVG(p.premium_annual) OVER (PARTITION BY ip.product_id) AS avg_premium_annual FROM policies p JOIN insurance_products ip ON p.product_id = ip.product_id) SELECT SUM(premium_annual) AS total_premium_annual FROM ranked_policies WHERE premium_annual > avg_premium_annual AND policy_id IN (SELECT policy_id FROM claims WHERE claim_amount > (SELECT AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims))

question: Find the top 3 policyholders with the highest total sum insured in each city, along with their total sum insured and rank within the city
sql: WITH ranked_holders AS (SELECT ph.holder_id, ph.city, SUM(p.sum_insured) AS total_sum_insured, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ph.city ORDER BY SUM(p.sum_insured) DESC) AS rank FROM policyholders ph JOIN policies p ON ph.holder_id = p.holder_id GROUP BY ph.holder_id, ph.city) SELECT city, holder_id, total_sum_insured, rank FROM ranked_holders WHERE rank <= 3 AND city IN (SELECT city FROM policyholders WHERE holder_id IN (SELECT holder_id FROM policies WHERE sum_insured > (SELECT AVG(sum_insured) FROM policies)))

question: List all insurance products with their average premium and the number of policies sold, ranked by the average premium within each product type
sql: WITH product_ranks AS (SELECT ip.product_id, ip.type, AVG(p.premium_annual) AS avg_premium, COUNT(p.policy_id) AS num_policies, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY ip.type ORDER BY AVG(p.premium_annual) DESC) AS rank FROM insurance_products ip JOIN policies p ON ip.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY ip.product_id, ip.type) SELECT product_id, type, avg_premium, num_policies, rank FROM product_ranks WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM policies WHERE premium_annual > (SELECT AVG(premium_annual) FROM policies))

question: Show me the total claim amount and the number of claims filed for each policyholder, along with their rank within their city based on the total claim amount
